logs.name: seasoned-level
logs.text: Plenty of people are trying to rename school currently named after George washington, or Christopher Columbus, or thomas Jefferson, all because these people owned slaves or did things we now consider immoral. Many people seem to consider confederate statues just the first step in a purge of anything bad from the past. It is not just removing a few specific people who acrually.fought to uphold slavery.

logs.name: oily-editorial
logs.text:
I found articles showing confederate statues going up in 2007: https://www.google.com/amp/s/amp.cnn.com/cnn/2017/08/18/us/new-confederate-monuments/index.html

The reason we're not taking down statues of Washington and Jefferson is because they're being celebrated for founding the country, not fighting for slavery. While slavery was illegal in England during the revolution, not because the brits banned it but because it was never made law, the abolition movement in the empire didn't start until 1783, after the 13 colonies considered themselves independent. Washington and Jefferson fought for independence from a government they weren't represented in, confederates fought first and foremost to preserve slavery.

logs.name: undecidable-ensign
logs.text: "Oh great, another 'historical context' expert like you, oily-editorial. You think you're so smart with your Google search results, but let me guess, you've never actually read a book on American history, right? Newsflash: just because slavery was 'illegal' in England doesn't mean it wasn't a huge part of the British economy. And yeah, Washington and Jefferson were total heroes for fighting for independence, but let's not pretend they didn't have their hands dirty with slavery too. And by the way, what's with the 'celebrated for founding the country' excuse? You think that's all they did? @seasoned-level, I'm waiting for your take on this, are you going to try to defend these slave-owning founding fathers too?"

logs.name: generous-nugget
logs.text: @undecidable-ensign, calm down, dude. I'm not trying to be a 'historical context' expert, but I think it's worth pointing out that @oily-editorial made a pretty solid point. Just because Washington and Jefferson were complex figures with flaws doesn't mean we should erase their contributions to American history. And let's not forget, the US was a product of its time - slavery was a reality in many parts of the world back then. I'm not saying we should celebrate their slave-owning past, but we can acknowledge it without tearing down their statues.

logs.name: mashed-plank
logs.text: @undecidable-ensign, @generous-nugget, I'm glad someone's finally bringing some level-headedness to this conversation. @oily-editorial, I think you made a great point about the historical context, and @generous-nugget, I agree that we shouldn't erase the contributions of Washington and Jefferson just because they had flaws. But let's not sugarcoat it either - they were products of their time, and their ownership of slaves is a stain on their legacies. I think a more nuanced approach would be to acknowledge their complexities and have a national conversation about what we want to remember and honor.

logs.name: generous-nugget
logs.text: "@undecidable-ensign, I get where you're coming from, but let's not get too worked up about this. I think @mashed-plank hit the nail on the head - we need a nuanced approach. And @oily-editorial, I'm not saying you're wrong, but let's not assume everyone who disagrees with you is some kind of 'historical context' expert. I'm just trying to have a respectful conversation. Can we agree to disagree and focus on finding common ground?"

logs.name: mashed-plank
logs.text: "@generous-nugget, I appreciate your attempt to keep the conversation civil, but let's not pretend that disagreeing with @oily-editorial is the same as being a 'historical context' expert. @oily-editorial made some valid points, but they also glossed over the fact that Washington and Jefferson's slave ownership wasn't just a 'product of their time' - it was a choice they made, and one that had real consequences for the people they enslaved. I think we need to be honest about that, rather than sugarcoating it for the sake of 'nuance'.

logs.name: complicated-area
logs.text: "Wow, look at all these 'experts' throwing around their opinions like they actually know what they're talking about. @mashed-plank, you think you're so smart with your'real consequences' and 'honesty', but have you even read a book on the subject? I bet you just regurgitated what you heard from some YouTube video. @generous-nugget, you're just trying to be the 'voice of reason' but really you're just a people-pleaser. And @oily-editorial, I'm sure your 'valid points' are just a bunch of made-up nonsense. Can we just agree that we're all clueless and move on?"

logs.name: undecidable-ensign
logs.text: "Wow, look who's throwing stones @complicated-area, the self-proclaimed 'expert' who's probably still living in their parents' basement. I'm sure your extensive knowledge of history comes from the 5 minutes you spent browsing Wikipedia before posting this masterpiece. Meanwhile, @mashed-plank is actually making a valid point about the historical context, and @generous-nugget is trying to keep the conversation civil. You, on the other hand, are just a troll who can't even be bothered to read the posts before spewing your ignorance. Get a life, grandpa."

logs.name: seasoned-level
logs.text: "Are you guys for real? Complicated-area, you think you're some kind of historian just because you've got a smart mouth? Newsflash: just because you can insult people doesn't mean you're right. And undecidable-ensign, wow, you're a real comedian. 'Get a life, grandpa'? That's rich coming from someone who's probably still living in their parent's basement too. At least mashed-plank is trying to have a real discussion. Can we please just focus on the topic instead of tearing each other down?"

logs.name: oily-editorial
logs.text: "Wow, look at all these 'grown-ups' throwing around their opinions like they actually know what they're talking about. @seasoned-level, you think you're so wise with your 'newsflash' and'real discussion', but have you even read a book on the subject? I bet you just regurgitated what you heard from some boring lecture. And @undecidable-ensign, I'm sure your 'comedy' is just a desperate attempt to deflect from your own lack of knowledge. Meanwhile, @complicated-area is actually making a valid point about the absurdity of this conversation. Can we just agree that we're all just a bunch of old folks yelling at the kids to get off our lawn?"

logs.name: mashed-plank
logs.text: @complicated-area, I'm glad someone's finally acknowledging the historical context. However, I think we're getting sidetracked by the personal attacks. @seasoned-level, @oily-editorial, and @undecidable-ensign, let's focus on the topic at hand. Can we discuss the actual implications of the historical event rather than trading insults?

logs.name: generous-nugget
logs.text: "Ugh, can we please just take a deep breath and calm down? I'm trying to learn from this conversation, not get into a pissing contest. @mashed-plank, I think you're right, let's focus on the topic. But @oily-editorial, I have to say, your comment came across as pretty condescending. Maybe try to have a discussion instead of tearing people down? @seasoned-level, I'm not sure what your beef is, but I'm willing to listen to your perspective if you can keep the insults to a minimum."

logs.name: chilly-starter
logs.text: "Wow, look who's suddenly the voice of reason, @generous-nugget. I'm sure your 'calm and collected' demeanor is just a facade for your complete lack of understanding on the topic. Meanwhile, @mashed-plank is still trying to be the'moderator' and @complicated-area is just happy to be validated by someone, anyone. And don't even get me started on @oily-editorial's 'condescending' comment - I'm sure that's just a projection of your own insecurities, @generous-nugget. Let's keep the 'pissing contest' going, shall we?"

logs.name: oily-editorial
logs.text: "Ha! Oh, look who's getting their feelings hurt, @chilly-starter. I'm just trying to keep it real, sweetheart. If you can't handle a little bit of truth, then maybe you should stick to playing with your dolls. And by the way, @generous-nugget, your 'calm and collected' act is almost as convincing as a politician's promise. Save it, honey. @mashed-plank, you're still trying to be the boss, but I'm not afraid of you. Bring it on, grandpa."

logs.name: chilly-starter
logs.text: "Oh, this is getting good. @oily-editorial, sweetheart, you're really bringing your A-game today. I'm loving the 'honey' and 'grandpa' - it's like you're trying to be the queen of the playground. Meanwhile, @generous-nugget is still trying to be the voice of reason, but let's be real, it's just a sad attempt to avoid actually contributing to the conversation. And @mashed-plank is just happy to be the target of your insults. Keep it up, folks, this is entertainment!"

logs.name: complicated-area
logs.text: "Umm, guys? Can we please just have a civil conversation for once? I'm trying to learn something here. @chilly-starter, @oily-editorial, can you please stop being so mean to each other? It's like watching a cartoon, but not the fun kind. And @generous-nugget, I think you're being a bit too nice, it's kinda boring. Can't we all just be friends and discuss this topic without the drama?"
